channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Manoj Kumar Agarwal appointed West Bengal Chief Secretary by Suvendu Adhikari government
item.description: The West Bengal government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, has appointed Manoj Kumar Agarwal, previously the state's Chief Electoral Officer (CEO), as the new Chief Secretary. Agarwal, a seni...

item.title: Passengers Evacuated After Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has resulted in multiple deaths and illnesses among passengers. Three people—a Dutch couple and a German woman—have died, while others, including an...

item.title: Northlands City and Tatu City Comparison: Rental Prices, Features and Investment Potential in Ruiru
item.description: Northlands City and Tatu City are two big private city projects coming up close to each other in Ruiru, Kiambu County. Northlands City, developed by the Kenyatta family, is a massive KSh 500 billion p...

item.title: China Develops GJ-21 Shipborne Stealth Drone for Naval Operations: Strategic Implications for India
item.description: The article discusses China's latest advancement in unmanned combat aerial vehicles (UCAVs) with the GJ-21, a shipborne variant of the GJ-11 Sharp Sword stealth drone. This naval drone features a flyi...

item.title: Ethiopia accuses Tigray leaders of forming new alliances amid rising tensions
item.description: Recently, the Ethiopian government say dem get proof say some leaders for Tigray state, wey dem don fight serious war with earlier this decade, dey try form alliance with foreign countries and local a...

item.title: ICC Issues Arrest Warrant Against Senator Bato dela Rosa Over Alleged Involvement in Davao Police Killings
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) confirmed on May 11, 2026, that it has issued an arrest warrant against Senator and former Philippine National Police chief Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa. This comes a...

item.title: All Western Cape schools closed as severe storm causes fatalities and flooding
item.description: All schools across the Western Cape have been closed due to severe weather conditions sweeping through the province, with at least one confirmed death and another person injured. The South African Wea...

item.title: National Assembly honours armed forces for response during May 2025 India conflict
item.description: On 11th May 2026, Pakistan’s National Assembly unanimously approved a resolution recognising the military’s decisive response to India’s unprovoked aggression during the May 2025 conflict, officially ...

item.title: Eight People Killed in Cult-Related Attack in Makurdi, Benue State
item.description: Early Monday morning, eight people were reportedly killed in different areas of High Level, Makurdi, Benue State, in what is suspected to be a cult-related attack. This comes just six days after anoth...

item.title: Barriers to Parenthood Persist for Gay Men in Canada Despite Legal Equality
item.description: Since the legalization of same-sex marriage in Canada in 2005, gay men have gained formal recognition as parents through changes in provincial adoption and parentage laws. However, research shows that...

item.title: Navodaya Vidyalaya Class 11 Admission Result 2026 Announced
item.description: The result for the Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya Lateral Entry Selection Test (JNV LEST) 2026 has been officially declared for students seeking admission into Class 11. Candidates who appeared for the ex...

item.title: Key Business Developments and Economic Updates to Watch This Week

item.title: Pakistan Summons Afghan Diplomat Over Bannu Police Post Suicide Attack
item.description: On May 9, 2026, a suicide attack targeted the Fateh Khel police post in Bannu District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, claiming the lives of 15 police personnel and injuring three others. The attackers drove an ...

item.title: Democratic primary tensions could jeopardize competitive Nebraska congressional race
item.description: A closely watched congressional race in Nebraska’s 2nd District is drawing national attention as Democrats see a rare opportunity to flip a historically Republican seat. The district, centered around ...

item.title: Tanzania U-20 Women’s Team Secures Spot in FIFA World Cup
item.description: Zanzibar witnessed a historic night for Tanzanian football as the U-20 women's national team, the Tanzanite Queens, staged a remarkable comeback to qualify for the 2026 FIFA U-20 Women's World Cup in ...

item.title: One Nation wins Farrer by-election as Liberals suffer historic swing
item.description: One Nation achieved a decisive victory in the Farrer by-election, marking a significant shift in the Australian political landscape. David Farley secured roughly 40% of first-preference votes, represe...

item.title: Prince Indah Shares Romantic Dance With Wife at Ajawa VVIP Experience
item.description: Prince Indah, the popular Ohangla musician, thrilled fans when he danced with his wife, Winnie Nyar Migori, on stage during the Ajawa VVIP Experience held at Golden Ice Runda. The couple’s chemistry w...

item.title: Rising Stock Market Concentration Driven by Tech Giants and AI
item.description: Stock market concentration is increasing globally, particularly in technology-heavy sectors, with a few dominant companies accounting for a large share of index values. In the U.S., the 'Magnificent S...

item.title: Bangsamoro groups support temporary halt on airport and seaport terminal fees
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Key business groups and advocacy organizations in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) have expressed support for a proposed temporary suspension of airport and ...

item.title: South Africa Declares National Disaster as Severe Weather Hits Multiple Provinces
item.description: South Africa has officially declared a national disaster following a week of severe weather affecting six provinces: Western Cape, Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, North West, Free State, and Mpumalanga. ...
